name: uart_rx
description: UART receiver that detects a start bit, samples serial data near bit centers, and emits received words with error flags.

source_refs.notes: AXI-stream-style UART receiver with overrun and frame error flags.

parameters.name: DATA_WIDTH
parameters.description: Number of payload bits to receive.

ports.name: overrun_error
ports.description: New byte arrived before previous byte was consumed.

ports.name: prescale
ports.description: Baud-rate prescale or bit timing control.

states.name: IDLE
states.description: Wait for rxd to go low.

states.name: START_CHECK
states.description: Delay half a bit and confirm start bit is still low.

states.name: DATA
states.description: Sample DATA_WIDTH data bits at bit centers.

states.name: STOP
states.description: Sample stop bit and raise valid or frame_error.

constraints: rxd must be treated as an external serial input and registered before control decisions.
Sample data near the center of each bit period, not immediately on the detected edge.
Hold output valid until the downstream ready handshake consumes the word.
Raise frame_error when the stop bit is sampled low.
patterns: Register the asynchronous-looking serial input before using it in control logic.
Use half-bit delay after falling edge detection to align to bit centers.
Shift sampled bits into a holding register LSB first.
Hold output valid until the downstream ready handshake consumes it.
implementation_notes: Detect a falling edge or low level on idle-high rxd, then wait half a bit before validating the start bit.
Use a prescale counter for sample timing and a bit counter for DATA_WIDTH payload bits.
Shift sampled serial bits into the receive register LSB first.
Gate overrun_error with an unconsumed previous output word.
test_strategy: Drive a legal UART frame and check m_axis_tdata/m_axis_tvalid.
Drive a low stop bit to check frame_error.
Hold m_axis_tready low across two frames to check overrun behavior.
verification_goals: A legal frame produces exactly one valid output word with the expected data.
Invalid stop bit raises frame_error and does not silently accept the byte.
Backpressure on the output can produce overrun_error on a second received byte.
busy is asserted only while a frame is being received.
